11   The function f is given by

                                              1
                                     f(x) = x 7 (x2 − x + 1)


     Find the fraction of the interval 0 < x < 1 for which f(x) is decreasing.

          2
     A
         15

         1
     B
         5

         1
     C
         3

         1
     D
         2

         2
     E
         3

         4
     F
         5

         13
     G
         15




                                             

stem.rawText:
The function f is given by

                                              1
                                     f(x) = x 7 (x2 − x + 1)


     Find the fraction of the interval 0 < x < 1 for which f(x) is decreasing.

          2

solution.rawText:
The function is decreasing when f ′ (x) < 0 (and possibly also at points where f ′ (x) = 0). We
have
                                  f(x) = x15/7 − x8/7 + x1/7
so

                                f ′ (x) = 15
                                          7 x
                                              8/7
                                                  − 87 x1/7 + 17 x−6/7
                                      = 17 x−6/7 (15x2 − 8x + 1)
                                      = 71 x−6/7 (5x − 1)(3x − 1)

This is negative when (5x − 1)(3x − 1) < 0 (note that we are only considering the region x > 0
here).
We note that (5x − 1)(3x − 1) = 0 exactly when x = 15 or x = 13 and is therefore negative when
1       1                                                  1       1
5 < x < 3 . Therefore the function f(x) is decreasing when 5 < x < 3 (and possibly also at the
endpoints of this interval).
Thus the fraction of the interval 0 < x < 1 for which f(x) is decreasing is 13 − 15 = 15
                                                                                      2
                                                                                         , which is
option A.
